results.evidence: Hono route businesses.ts:274 already had LIMIT_REACHED check — existing code
Astro route create.ts:33-40 added LIMIT_REACHED check
hasUserBusiness uses eq(businessPages.ownerId, userId) — uses business_owner_idx
Astro route create.ts:12-25 uses auth.api.getSession() with cookie header
create.astro:8-30 alert card shown via showExistingBusinessAlert()
getSession() called first (line 362), then my-business check (line 368-383)
businesses.ts:61-65 returns 404 when no business found
businesses.ts:52 uses getCurrentUser(c) from session cookie
hasUserBusiness() returns { id, title, slug, status } — minimal payload

findings.title: API-layer unit tests for LIMIT_REACHED not implemented
findings.issue: AC-US4-01 through AC-US4-04 require unit tests for the API-layer behavior (GET /my-business 401/404/200, POST /api/businesses/create 401/LIMIT_REACHED/201). Only TC-001 and TC-002 (pure function) are implemented. TC-003 through TC-008 are missing.
findings.suggestion: Add route-level tests using Hono's c.req mock or Astro's API route test helpers. Mock getCurrentUser() and hasUserBusiness() to test the handler logic. Alternatively, add integration tests in e2e/business-complete.spec.ts to cover these scenarios.
findings.impact: Without API-layer tests, a future refactor that breaks session auth or LIMIT_REACHED in create.ts would not be caught by CI. The Hono route's limit check is protected by existing behavior, but the Astro route's new auth + limit logic has no regression safety net.
